subject: UX & Research
title: Hofstede's Cultural Dimensions in UX
description: A quick win: understand Hofstede's Cultural Dimensions in UX.

hook.text: Japanese websites are dense with information. American sites are spacious. Geert Hofstede's research explains why.

buildup.text: Hofstede identified cultural dimensions like individualism vs. collectivism and uncertainty avoidance in the 1980s.

discovery.text: High uncertainty-avoidance cultures prefer detailed, information-rich pages. They want all facts before deciding.

twist.text: Low uncertainty-avoidance cultures prefer clean, minimal designs. They're comfortable deciding with less information.

climax.text: Global products need cultural research, not just translation. Layout, density, and flow preferences vary by culture.

punchline.text: Culture shapes layout preferences. Clean to one feels empty to another.

quiz.question: Why do Japanese websites tend to be more information-dense than American ones?
options: High uncertainty-avoidance cultures prefer seeing all information before making decisions
American designers are simply more minimalist by training
